content.body:
A few updates from the in-person WHATWG WHATNOT meeting that may be of interest to TG2:
- [Spell check custom dictionary](https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12590) advanced to Stage 2
- [Localized time formatting without JavaScript](https://github.com/whatwg/html/issues/12591) was approved for Stage 1. This is proposing to add attributes corresponding to the Unicode MessageFormat datetime formatting options to the HTML `<time>` element, such that the rendered contents of the element would become a localized representation of the element's `datetime` attribute value.
- [DOM Localization](https://github.com/whatwg/html/issues/12584) was approved for Stage 1.

m.new_content.formatted_body:
A few updates from the in-person WHATWG WHATNOT meeting at the Web Engines Hackfest that may be of interest to TG2:

  • Spell check custom dictionary advanced to Stage 2
  • Localized time formatting without JavaScript was approved for Stage 1. This is proposing to add attributes corresponding to the Unicode MessageFormat datetime formatting options to the HTML <time> element, such that the rendered contents of the element would become a localized representation of the element's datetime attribute value.

content.body: Luca Casonato is championing the `<time>` element work, and we'll likely be bringing its proposed API for consideration for Intl.DateTimeFormat as well. The MF2 formatting options can be mapped directly to a subset of the Intl.DTF constructor options, so this is not really asking for any new capabilities, just a different way of accessing them.
content.formatted_body: Luca Casonato is championing the <time> element work, and we'll likely be bringing its proposed API for consideration for Intl.DateTimeFormat as well. The MF2 formatting options can be mapped directly to a subset of the Intl.DTF constructor options, so this is not really asking for any new capabilities, just a different way of accessing them.
